decision_summary: The design is feasible with a chip max temperature of ~58.6°C, well below the 85.0°C constraint. To further minimize the objective, I will increase the heat spreader width to enhance lateral heat distribution towards the cooled boundaries.

changes.reason: Increasing the spreader width maximizes the lateral area available for heat conduction towards the left and right Dirichlet boundaries, reducing thermal resistance.

expected_effects: More uniform temperature distribution in the spreader layer
risk_notes: Approaching the upper geometric bound (1.0m) limits future width increases
Diminishing returns may occur if the base plate conductivity becomes the new bottleneck
